original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Can DoS All Swaps
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `PairFactory.setReferralFee` function lacks an upper bound check. If a compromised or malicious admin sets the fee > 10000, the fee calculation in `Pair` will underflow, permanently reverting all swaps.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In `Pair._update0`, `_referralFee = amount * _maxRef / 10000`. If `_maxRef > 10000`, `_referralFee` exceeds `amount`. The subsequent `amount -= _referralFee` will trigger an arithmetic underflow, reverting the transaction. This allows a single admin action to completely halt trading across the entire protocol.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing upper bound validation on fee setter

preconditions: FeeManager role is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Causes underflow in core trading logic

analysis_questions: Is the FeeManager role secured by a timelock or multisig?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An attacker compromises the FeeManager key and calls `setReferralFee(10001)`. All subsequent swaps on all pairs instantly revert due to underflow, causing a complete protocol denial of service.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete halt of protocol trading revenue
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires admin to reset the fee to a valid value

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing boundary tests for fee setters

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible via admin transaction setting invalid fee

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
